title: Calculating PK parameters of simulation outputs
text: Population analysis calculated R can exported *.csv file loaded PK-Sim, vice versa.
code: # Load and run the simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) simulationResults <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)[[1]]  # Calculate PK-analysis pkAnalysis <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults)  # Export to csv csvPKAnalysisPath <- system.file("extdata", "PKAnalyses.csv", package = "ospsuite") exportPKAnalysesToCSV(pkAnalyses = pkAnalysis, filePath = csvPKAnalysisPath)  # Load from csv pkAnalysisLoaded <- importPKAnalysesFromCSV(filePath = csvPKAnalysisPath, simulation = sim)

title: Calculating PK parameters of simulation outputs
text: ospsuite package also supports user-defined PK Parameters, e.g.┬ĀPK Parameter can tailored specific project needs. feature useful calculating PK Parameters specific time intervals, apply PK Parameters output using dimension Concentration. User-Defined PK Parameter always based existing PK-Parameter simply extends way output calculated. possible moment define formula. utility addUserDefinedPKParameter creates returns instance UserDefinedPKParameter can parameterized specific requirements.

what: Calculate AUC between for a specific time interval.
title: Calculating PK parameters of simulation outputs
code: # Adds a user defined parameter named `MyAuc` that will calculate the value of AUC between t=50 min and t=80min only.  # Create a new parameter based on the standard AUC parameter myAUC <- addUserDefinedPKParameter(   name = "MyAUC",   standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$AUC_tEnd )  # Specifies start time and end time in minute myAUC$startTime <- 50 myAUC$endTime <- 80

what: Calculate CMax between the 4th and 5th application of a multiple applications simulation.
title: Calculating PK parameters of simulation outputs
code: # Adds a user defined parameter named `MyCMax` that will calculate the value of Cmax between the 4th and 5th application  # Create a new parameter based on the standard C_max parameter myCMax <- addUserDefinedPKParameter(   name = "MyCMax",   standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$C_max )  # Specifies start application (4th) and end application (5th) myCMax$startApplicationIndex <- 4 myCMax$endApplicationIndex <- 5

what: Calculate CMax between the 4th and 5th application of a multiple applications simulation and apply a time offset
title: Calculating PK parameters of simulation outputs
code: # Adds a user defined parameter named `MyCMax` that will calculate the value of Cmax between the 4th application start time + 10 min and # the 5th application start time + 20min  # Create a new parameter based on the standard C_max parameter myCMax <- addUserDefinedPKParameter(   name = "MyCMax",   standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$C_max )  # Specifies start application (4th) and end application (5th) myCMax$startApplicationIndex <- 4 myCMax$endApplicationIndex <- 5  # Specifies start time offset. The PK calculations will effectively start at StartTime of 4th Application + 10 min myCMax$startTimeOffset <- 10  # Specifies end time offset. The PK calculations will effectively ends at StartTime of 5th Application + 20 min myCMax$endTimeOffset <- 20

what: Update Dimension of a standard parameter for correct visualization
title: Calculating PK parameters of simulation outputs
text: PK parameters currently always assume underlying quantity concentration check units. quantity interest another dimension, creating user-defined PK parameter setting display unit valid unit target dimension can trick happen : dimension user defined PK Parameter estimated display unit, e.g case mg/m┬▓ calculated value user defined PK Parameter interpreted value base unit dimension, e.g.┬ĀŌĆ£kg/dm┬▓ŌĆØ display unit differs base unit dimension, calculated value converted target display unit E.g. example : First, C_max value quantity calculated (user defined parameter derived C_max) display unit mg/m┬▓, dimension estimated (ŌĆ£Dose per body surface areaŌĆØ) calculated C_max value interpreted value kg/dm┬▓ Finally, calculated value converted mg/m┬▓ set user defined PK Parameter. see options user defined PK Parameter, refer documentation UserDefinedPKParameter (?UserDefinedPKParameter RStudio)
code: # Let's assume there is an observer called Q_observer in mg/m2 using the dimension Dose per body surface area. # Simply using C_max would result in the parameter being shown in umol\l. # To see the correct unit and dimension, the following can be done:  QMax <- addUserDefinedPKParameter(   name = "Q_max",   standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$C_max,   displayName = "Q max",   displayUnit = "mg/m┬▓" )

what: Running individual simulation and retrieving the results
title: Running a simulation
text: simulation loaded (see Loading simulation accessing entities), can run using runSimulations() produce object class SimulationResults. Keep mind runSimulations() produces list SimulationResults objects. advantage storing results object option keep different results simulation produced different settings (e.g., model parameters). Simulated time-value pairs specific output SimulationResults-object can accessed method getOutputValues. user can provide either path(s) output (can molecule, parameter, observer), object(s) type Molecule, Parameter, Quantity (observers) argument quantitiesOrPaths. output specified, outputs available simulation results returned. paths available outputs can accessed via getOutputValues returns list two entries: data metadata: data dataframe two predefined columns (IndividualId Time) well one column requested output IndividualId (relevant individual simulation) Time vector simulated time values (minutes, equal outputs) vector simulated entries output requested. metaData list containing one entry requested output. entry contains information pertinent output dimension unit.  results can stored imported *.csv file methods exportResultsToCSV importResultsFromCSV.
code: library(ospsuite)  # Load the simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  simulationResults <- runSimulations(simulations = sim) # Extract `SimulationResults` by simulation id simulationResults <- simulationResults[[sim$id]] print(simulationResults) #> <SimulationResults> #>   ŌĆó Number of individuals: 1 #> For paths: #>   ŌĆó Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) simulationResults$allQuantityPaths #> [1] "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" # Get simulated results by path resultsPath <- simulationResults$allQuantityPaths[[1]] print(resultsPath) #> [1] "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"  resultsData <- getOutputValues(   simulationResults,   quantitiesOrPaths = resultsPath )  resultsTime <- resultsData$data$Time resultsValues <- resultsData$data$`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)`  plot(resultsTime, resultsValues, type = "l") # Load and run the simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) # `runSimulations` returns a list of `SimulationResults`. For single simulation, # we directly extract the first element, as only one object is created. simulationResults <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)[[1]]  # Export to csv csvResultsPath <- system.file("extdata", "SimResults.csv", package = "ospsuite") exportResultsToCSV(results = simulationResults, filePath = csvResultsPath)  # Load from csv resultsLoaded <- importResultsFromCSV(   filePaths = csvResultsPath,   simulation = sim ) print(resultsLoaded) #> <SimulationResults> #>   ŌĆó Number of individuals: 1 #> For paths: #>   ŌĆó Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)

what: Running multiple individual simulations and retrieving the results
title: Running a simulation
text: cases, user might want run multiple simulations parallel. can achieved easily simply passing list simulations runSimulations() function. However, individual simulations (.e., population argument must remain empty) supported. default, simulations executed parallel using cores available machine minus 1. 8 cores user simulates 4 simulations, 4 cores used. hand, user simulates 10 simulations 8 cores available, 7 used first 3 available. results case named list SimulationResults-object , .e.┬Āone simulation. names entries list ids simulation results correspond. way, easy retrieve correct results specific simulation
code: # Load and run multiple simulations concurrently. simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")  # We load 3 times the same simulation for convenience. But in real life scenarios, # they should be different simulations sim1 <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) sim2 <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) sim3 <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  simulationResults <- runSimulations(simulations = list(sim1, sim2, sim3)) # Get the id of the second simulation id <- sim2$id print(id) #> [1] "1T9iqs5Bm0KLfsGOtwGeLA" # get the corresponding result sim2Results <- simulationResults[[id]] print(sim2Results$simulation$id) #> [1] "1T9iqs5Bm0KLfsGOtwGeLA"

title: Running a simulation
text: default, outputs selected PK-Sim MoBi prior export simulation pkml generated. user can add new outputs simulation method addOutputs. outputs can provided either objects type(s) Molecule, Parameter, Quantity, path strings. output list property simulation. adding removing outputs, corresponding simulation needs re-run order generate updated results. clearOutputs() addOutputs() can combined function setOutputs().
code: # Clear the list of generated outputs clearOutputs(sim)  # Add new outputs as objects molecule <- getMolecule("Organism|Kidney|Intracellular|Aciclovir", sim) observer <- getQuantity("Organism|Lumen|Aciclovir|Fraction dissolved", sim)  addOutputs(c(molecule, observer), simulation = sim)  # Add new outputs as path strings addOutputs(   c(     "Organism|Lumen|Stomach|Aciclovir",     "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)"   ),   simulation = sim )  # Run simulation simulationResults <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)  # Retrieve all generated outputs (e.g. omitting the quantitiesOrPaths property # will return all available values) resultsData <- getOutputValues(simulationResults)  # Note that "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" # is not in the list of generated results any more names(resultsData$data) #> NULL

title: Running a simulation
text: simulation interval (.e., simulation times results stored) stored property $outputSchema Simulation object. change simulation interval, user can use one functions clearOutputIntervals(), addOutputInterval(), setOutputInterval().
code: print(sim$outputSchema) #> <OutputSchema> #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Output intervals ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #> <Interval> #>   ŌĆó Name: Simulation interval high resolution #>   ŌĆó Start time: 0.00e+00 [min] #>   ŌĆó End time: 15.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó Resolution: 1.00 [pts/min] #> <Interval> #>   ŌĆó Name: Simulation Interval 1 #>   ŌĆó Start time: 15.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó End time: 1440.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó Resolution: 0.33 [pts/min] #> <Interval> #>   ŌĆó Name: Simulation Interval 2 #>   ŌĆó Start time: 120.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó End time: 1440.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó Resolution: 0.07 [pts/min] # Remove all output intervals - simulation not possible! clearOutputIntervals(simulation = sim) runSimulations(simulations = sim) #> Error in `do.call()`: #> ! Type:    OSPSuite.Utility.Exceptions.OSPSuiteException #> Message: Time points output schema is empty #> Method:  Void EvaluateCppCallResult(Boolean, System.String) #> Stack trace: #>    at OSPSuite.SimModel.PInvokeHelper.EvaluateCppCallResult(Boolean success, String errorMessage) #>    at OSPSuite.SimModel.Simulation.evaluateCppCallResult(Boolean success, String errorMessage) #>    at OSPSuite.SimModel.Simulation.RunSimulation() #>    at OSPSuite.Core.Domain.Services.SimModelManager.simulate() #>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.RunFromThreadPoolDispatchLoop(Thread threadPoolThread, ExecutionContext executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state) #> --- End of stack trace from previous location --- #>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.RunFromThreadPoolDispatchLoop(Thread threadPoolThread, ExecutionContext executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state) #>    at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.ExecuteWithThreadLocal(Task& currentTaskSlot, Thread threadPoolThread) #> --- End of stack trace fr  # Add an interval addOutputInterval(   simulation = sim,   startTime = 0,   endTime = 20,   resolution = 60,   intervalName = "highRes" ) print(sim$outputSchema) #> <OutputSchema> #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Output intervals ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #> <Interval> #>   ŌĆó Name: highRes #>   ŌĆó Start time: 0.00e+00 [min] #>   ŌĆó End time: 20.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó Resolution: 60.00 [pts/min]  # Add a second interval addOutputInterval(   simulation = sim,   startTime = 30,   endTime = 2000,   resolution = 4,   intervalName = "lowRes" ) print(sim$outputSchema) #> <OutputSchema> #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Output intervals ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #> <Interval> #>   ŌĆó Name: highRes #>   ŌĆó Start time: 0.00e+00 [min] #>   ŌĆó End time: 20.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó Resolution: 60.00 [pts/min] #> <Interval> #>   ŌĆó Name: lowRes #>   ŌĆó Start time: 30.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó End time: 2000.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó Resolution: 4.00 [pts/min]  # Replace the existing interval(s) with a new one setOutputInterval(   simulation = sim,   startTime = 0,   endTime = 2000,   resolution = 4 ) print(sim$outputSchema) #> <OutputSchema> #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Output intervals ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #> <Interval> #>   ŌĆó Name: Output interval #>   ŌĆó Start time: 0.00e+00 [min] #>   ŌĆó End time: 2000.00 [min] #>   ŌĆó Resolution: 4.00 [pts/min]

title: Running a simulation
text: solver settings control numerical integration algorithm used solve differential equations simulation. settings can accessed modified $solver property Simulation object. cases, simulation may fail run successfully due numerical instability convergence issues. One common approach resolve issues reduce solver tolerances, makes solver use smaller step sizes precise calculations. two main tolerance parameters : absTol: Absolute tolerance - controls accuracy solution absolute terms relTol: Relative tolerance - controls accuracy solution relative magnitude values solver settings can adjusted include: h0: Initial time step size hMin: Minimum step size allowed hMax: Maximum step size allowed mxStep: Maximum number (internal) steps taken solver attempt reach next output time useJacobian: Indicates whether use analytically calculated Jacobian matrix calculations. disabled, approximation difference quotients used, much slower majority cases details solver properties effects, see MoBi documentation ODE Solver Properties.
code: # View current solver settings print(sim$solver) #> <SolverSettings> #>   ŌĆó useJacobian: TRUE #>   ŌĆó h0: 1e-10 #>   ŌĆó hMin: 0 #>   ŌĆó hMax: 60 #>   ŌĆó mxStep: 100000 #>   ŌĆó relTol: 1e-05 #>   ŌĆó absTol: 1e-10 # If a simulation fails to run, try reducing the tolerances # Reduce absolute tolerance by a factor of 10 sim$solver$absTol <- sim$solver$absTol * 1e-1  # Optionally, the relative tolerance can also be reduced # sim$solver$relTol <- sim$solver$relTol * 1e-1  # After changing solver settings, re-run the simulation simulationResults <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)[[1]]

title: Sensitivity analysis
text: first step performing sensitivity analysis creating object class SensitivityAnalysis. step, user can define parameters considered sensitivity analysis, range values varied, may steps performed one direction (plus minus). parameters specified, constant suitable sensitivity calculations parameters simulation varied. list parameters paths simulation can accessed method potentialVariableParameterPathsFor(simulation).

title: Sensitivity analysis
text: numberOfSteps parameter specifies number steps one direction (either positive negative) reference parameter value. Since parameters varied directions, total number variations per parameter 2 * numberOfSteps. variationRange parameter defines total relative range variation (e.g., 0.1 means ┬▒10%). range divided equal intervals based number steps. Example: numberOfSteps = 2 variationRange = 0.1 (default values): - parameter tested four points: 90%, 95%, 105%, 110% reference value - Total number simulations: 2 * numberOfSteps * number_of_parameters Example: numberOfSteps = 1 variationRange = 0.1: - parameter tested two points: 90% 110% reference value New parameters can added existing SensitivityAnalysis calling method addParameterPaths(). NOTE: - parameters specified creation SensitivityAnalysis, constant suitable sensitivity calculation parameters considered. - cases, calling addParameterPaths() vary newly added parameters. run specified SensitivityAnalysis, call method runSensitivityAnalysis(). method returns object class SensitivityAnalysisResults. impact defined parameters calculated PK-Parameters (see PK Analysis information) model outputs. method allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor() returns list PKParameterSensitivity objects. PKParameterSensitivity describes sensitivity (field $value) PK-Parameter ($pkParameterName) output $outputPath calculated varied parameter $parameterPath. argument totalSensitivityThreshold method allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor() used filter impactful parameters. threshold 0.9 means parameters participating total 90 percent sensitivity returned. value 1 return sensitivity parameters. value provided, default value used.
code: library(ospsuite)  # Load simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "simple.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # Get the paths of parameters that will be considered by default. potentialSAParameters <- potentialVariableParameterPathsFor(sim) print(potentialSAParameters) #> [1] "Organism|Volume" "Organism|Q"      "R1|k1"  # Create a default `SensitivityAnalysis` for the simulation sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(simulation = sim) print(sa) #> <SensitivityAnalysis> #>   ŌĆó Number of steps: 2 #>   ŌĆó Variation range: 0.1 #>   ŌĆó Number of parameters to vary: Will be estimated at run time  # Create a `SensitivityAnalysis` with specified parameters sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(simulation = sim, parameterPaths = c("Organism|Q", "Organism|Volume")) print(sa) #> <SensitivityAnalysis> #>   ŌĆó Number of steps: 2 #>   ŌĆó Variation range: 0.1 #>   ŌĆó Number of parameters to vary: 2  # Show which parameters will be varied sa$parameterPaths #> [1] "Organism|Q"      "Organism|Volume" library(ospsuite)  # Load simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "simple.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # Create a `SensitivityAnalysis` with specified parameters sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(simulation = sim, parameterPaths = c(   "Organism|Q",   "Organism|Volume" )) # Add new parameter sa$addParameterPaths("R1|k1")  # Show which parameters will be varied sa$parameterPaths #> [1] "Organism|Q"      "Organism|Volume" "R1|k1" # Load simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "simple.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # Create a `SensitivityAnalysis` with all constant and suitable for sensitivity calculations parameters and suitable for sensitivity calculations parameters sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(simulation = sim)  # Run the sensitivity analysis saResult <- runSensitivityAnalysis(sa) print(saResult) #> <SensitivityAnalysisResults> #>   ŌĆó Number of calculated sensitivities: 132 #> Available PK Parameters: #>   ŌĆó C_max #>   ŌĆó t_max #>   ŌĆó C_tEnd #>   ŌĆó AUC_tEnd #>   ŌĆó AUC_inf #>   ŌĆó MRT #>   ŌĆó Thalf #>   ŌĆó FractionAucLastToInf #>   ŌĆó CL #>   ŌĆó Vss #>   ŌĆó Vd #> For the following outputs: #>   ŌĆó Organism|B #>   ŌĆó Organism|A #>   ŌĆó Organism|Liver|A #>   ŌĆó Organism|Liver|B

title: Sensitivity analysis
text: default values numberOfSteps, variationRange, totalSensitivityThreshold can retrieved using getOSPSuiteSetting("sensitivityAnalysisConfig"). values can changed session directly setting values ospsuiteEnv: Note changes affect new SensitivityAnalysis objects created change. Existing objects retain original values. value -1 sensitivity ŌĆ£AUC_infŌĆØ output Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) parameter Organism-Kidney-Volume means increasing Organism-Kidney-Volume 10% decrease ŌĆ£AUC_infŌĆØ 10%. Note list sensitivities ordered largest smallest respect magnitude.
code: # View current defaults getOSPSuiteSetting("sensitivityAnalysisConfig")  # Change the defaults for the current R session ospsuiteEnv$sensitivityAnalysisConfig$numberOfSteps <- 3 ospsuiteEnv$sensitivityAnalysisConfig$variationRange <- 0.2 ospsuiteEnv$sensitivityAnalysisConfig$totalSensitivityThreshold <- 0.95 # Load simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # Default value of the threshold getOSPSuiteSetting("sensitivityAnalysisConfig")$totalSensitivityThreshold #> [1] 0.9  # Create a `SensitivityAnalysis` with all constant parameters and run it sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(   simulation = sim,   parameterPaths = c(     "Aciclovir|Lipophilicity",     "Aciclovir|Fraction unbound (plasma)",     "Organism|Age"   ) ) saResult <- runSensitivityAnalysis(sa) print(saResult) #> <SensitivityAnalysisResults> #>   ŌĆó Number of calculated sensitivities: 11 #> Available PK Parameters: #>   ŌĆó C_max #>   ŌĆó t_max #>   ŌĆó C_tEnd #>   ŌĆó AUC_tEnd #>   ŌĆó AUC_inf #>   ŌĆó MRT #>   ŌĆó Thalf #>   ŌĆó FractionAucLastToInf #>   ŌĆó CL #>   ŌĆó Vss #>   ŌĆó Vd #> For the following outputs: #>   ŌĆó Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)  # Get sensitivities for the parameter "AUC_inf" of the simulated output with a threshold of 0.8 outputPath <- sim$outputSelections$allOutputs[[1]]$path sensitivities <- saResult$allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor(pkParameterName = "AUC_inf", outputPath = outputPath, totalSensitivityThreshold = 0.8) print(sensitivities) #> [[1]] #> <PKParameterSensitivity> #>   ŌĆó Parameter name: Aciclovir-Lipophilicity #>   ŌĆó PK-Parameter: AUC_inf #>   ŌĆó Output path: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral #>   Venous Blood) #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.00470852808265833

what: Import and export of sensitivity analysis results
title: Sensitivity analysis
text: Sensitivity analysis calculated R can exported *.csv file, can loaded another instance.
code: # Load and run the simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "simple.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) simulationResults <- runSimulations(simulations = sim)[[1]]  # Create a `SensitivityAnalysis` with all constant parameters and run it sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(simulation = sim) saResult <- runSensitivityAnalysis(sa)  # Export to csv saResultPath <- system.file("extdata", "SAResult.csv", package = "ospsuite") exportSensitivityAnalysisResultsToCSV(results = saResult, filePath = saResultPath)  # Load from csv saResultLoaded <- importSensitivityAnalysisResultsFromCSV(filePaths = saResultPath, simulation = sim)

title: Changing parameter and molecule start values
text: possible change initial values parameters molecules simulation. important differentiate constant values values defined formula.

title: Changing parameter and molecule start values
text: Every (initial) value described either constant formula. value defined simple constant, field isConstant respective parameter molecule value TRUE. value constant, described one formula types: Distributed: Distributed parameters describe variation around constant value two numerical limits. simulation, behavior equals constant values. See OSPS documentation information. Explicit formula: value parameter given explicit formula. value explicit formula can change simulation. string formula can accessed via parameter$formulaString. Table formula: value parameter given table x-y value pairs. X values usually refer simulation time. See Table parameters additional information retrieve change table values. Additionally, parameters modeled state variables. case, parameter initial value right hand side (RHS) formula, can formula type.
code: library(ospsuite)  # Load simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # Get the parameter "Age" of the Organism ageParam <- getParameter("Organism|Age", sim) print(ageParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Age #>   ŌĆó Value: 25.70 [year(s)] #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE ageParam$isConstant #> [1] TRUE # Get the parameter "Volume" of the Liver liverVolume <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Volume", sim) print(liverVolume) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 2.17 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-ICRP, 2002. Basic Anatomical and Physiological #>   Data for Use in Radiological Protection Reference Values. ICRP Publication #>   89. Ann. ICRP 32 (3-4). https://doi.org/10.1016/0146-6453(79)90123-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isDistributed: TRUE liverVolume$isDistributed #> [1] TRUE # Get the parameter "Volume" of the Liver interstital liverIntVolume <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume", sim) print(liverIntVolume) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.35 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-Willmann S, Lippert J, Sevestre M, Solodenko J, #>   Fois F, Schmitt W. PK-Sim┬«: a physiologically based pharmacokinetic #>   ŌĆśwhole-bodyŌĆÖ model. Biosilico. 2003; 1 (4): 121-124. #>   http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S1478-5382%2803%2902342-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: f_int*V #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: FALSE liverIntVolume$formulaString #> [1] "f_int*V" # Get the parameter defined by a table. tableParam <- getParameter("Organism|TableParameter", sim) print(tableParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|TableParameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.00e+00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isTable: TRUE #>   ŌĆó XDimension: Time #>   ŌĆó UseDerivedValues: FALSE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Table values ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ #>   x= 0, y= 0, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 60, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 120, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 180, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: FALSE # Get the parameter defined by a state variable. stateVariableParam <- getParameter("Organism|StateVariable_Parameter", sim) print(stateVariableParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|StateVariable_Parameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.00e+00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ State variable ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isStateVariable: TRUE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ RHSFormula  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: Time * 2  # `value` refers to the initial value of the parameter stateVariableParam$value #> [1] 0 # `rhsFormula` is the right hand side of the parameter stateVariableParam$rhsFormula #> <Formula> #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: Time * 2

what: Changing parameters and molecules initial values
title: Changing parameter and molecule start values
text: user can change initial values parameters molecules methods setParameterValues setMoleculeInitialValues, respectively. Another way change parameter values scale . scaling always performed relative current value: constant values can set. parameters value defined formula (explicit table), newly assigned value override formula. reflected field isFixedValue. Changing values formula parameters done caution, potential dependency another simulation parameters destroyed. parameter value can reset formula assigning constant value: Changing value state variable parameter change initial value, right hand side (.e., value simulation still change according RHS formula). switch RHS formula , , make parameter dependent initial value, field isStateVariable can set FALSE. function one-way ! possible re-activate RHS formula switching . example set initial values molecules containers certain value:
code: # Get the parameter Dose doseParamPath <- "Applications|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose" doseParam <- getParameter(doseParamPath, sim) print(doseParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Applications|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose #>   ŌĆó Value: 2.50e-04 [kg] #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE  # Change the dose to 350mg. The value has to be converted to base unit, first newValue <- toBaseUnit(quantity = doseParam, values = 350, unit = "mg") setParameterValues(parameters = doseParam, values = newValue) print(doseParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Applications|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose #>   ŌĆó Value: 3.50e-04 [kg] #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE doseParamPath <- "Applications|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose" doseParam <- getParameter(doseParamPath, sim) print(doseParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Applications|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose #>   ŌĆó Value: 3.50e-04 [kg] #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE  # Double the dose scaleParameterValues(doseParam, factor = 2) print(doseParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Applications|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose #>   ŌĆó Value: 7.00e-04 [kg] #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE  # Half the dose scaleParameterValues(doseParam, factor = 0.5) print(doseParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Applications|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose #>   ŌĆó Value: 3.50e-04 [kg] #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE # Get the parameter "Volume" of the Liver interstital liverIntVolume <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume", sim) print(liverIntVolume) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.35 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-Willmann S, Lippert J, Sevestre M, Solodenko J, #>   Fois F, Schmitt W. PK-Sim┬«: a physiologically based pharmacokinetic #>   ŌĆśwhole-bodyŌĆÖ model. Biosilico. 2003; 1 (4): 121-124. #>   http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S1478-5382%2803%2902342-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: f_int*V #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: FALSE  # isFixedValue is FALSE as the parameter is defined by its formula liverIntVolume$isFixedValue #> [1] FALSE  setParameterValues(liverIntVolume, 1) print(liverIntVolume) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 1.00 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-Willmann S, Lippert J, Sevestre M, Solodenko J, #>   Fois F, Schmitt W. PK-Sim┬«: a physiologically based pharmacokinetic #>   ŌĆśwhole-bodyŌĆÖ model. Biosilico. 2003; 1 (4): 121-124. #>   http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S1478-5382%2803%2902342-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: f_int*V #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: TRUE # isFixedValue is TRUE as the value of parameter is overridden by the constant value liverIntVolume$isFixedValue #> [1] TRUE print(liverIntVolume) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 1.00 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-Willmann S, Lippert J, Sevestre M, Solodenko J, #>   Fois F, Schmitt W. PK-Sim┬«: a physiologically based pharmacokinetic #>   ŌĆśwhole-bodyŌĆÖ model. Biosilico. 2003; 1 (4): 121-124. #>   http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S1478-5382%2803%2902342-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: f_int*V #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: TRUE  # isFixedValue is TRUE as the value of parameter is overridden by the constant value liverIntVolume$isFixedValue #> [1] TRUE  liverIntVolume$reset() print(liverIntVolume) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.35 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-Willmann S, Lippert J, Sevestre M, Solodenko J, #>   Fois F, Schmitt W. PK-Sim┬«: a physiologically based pharmacokinetic #>   ŌĆśwhole-bodyŌĆÖ model. Biosilico. 2003; 1 (4): 121-124. #>   http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S1478-5382%2803%2902342-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: f_int*V #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: FALSE liverIntVolume$isFixedValue #> [1] FALSE # Get the parameter defined by a state variable. stateVariableParam <- getParameter("Organism|StateVariable_Parameter", sim) print(stateVariableParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|StateVariable_Parameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.00e+00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ State variable ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isStateVariable: TRUE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ RHSFormula  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: Time * 2  # Setting its value only changes the initial value setParameterValues(stateVariableParam, 10) print(stateVariableParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|StateVariable_Parameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 10.00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ State variable ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isStateVariable: TRUE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ RHSFormula  #>   ŌĆó isExplicit: TRUE #>   ŌĆó formula: Time * 2  # Switching the RHS formula off stateVariableParam$isStateVariable <- FALSE print(stateVariableParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|StateVariable_Parameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 10.00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE  # Switching it on is not supported stateVariableParam$isStateVariable <- TRUE #> Error: #> ! Creating a RHS Formula is not supported at the moment. This should be done in MoBi. # Get objects representing the molecule Aciclovir in all containers allAciclovirMolecules <- getAllMoleculesMatching("Organism|**|Aciclovir", sim)  # Set initial values to 10 ┬Ąmol in all containers setMoleculeInitialValues(allAciclovirMolecules, rep(10, length(allAciclovirMolecules)))

title: Table parameters
text: Simply setting value table-defined parameter using setParameterValues override formula make parameter constant. change values table, set methods TableFormula available. method addPoints() adds set x-y values existing table. trying add point x-value already present table, error thrown: remove point table, use method removePoint(). remove point x value present table provided y. clearPoints() method removes points table, setPoints() method combination clearing table adding new points:
code: # Get the parameter defined by a table. tableParam <- getParameter("Organism|TableParameter", sim) tableParam #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|TableParameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 0.00e+00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isTable: TRUE #>   ŌĆó XDimension: Time #>   ŌĆó UseDerivedValues: FALSE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Table values ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ #>   x= 0, y= 0, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 60, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 120, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 180, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: FALSE  # Set value to a constant. tableParam$isFixedValue is now TRUE setParameterValues(tableParam, 10) tableParam #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|TableParameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 10.00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isTable: TRUE #>   ŌĆó XDimension: Time #>   ŌĆó UseDerivedValues: FALSE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Table values ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ #>   x= 0, y= 0, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 60, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 120, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 180, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: TRUE tableParam <- getParameter("Organism|TableParameter", sim) tableParam #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|TableParameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 10.00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isTable: TRUE #>   ŌĆó XDimension: Time #>   ŌĆó UseDerivedValues: FALSE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Table values ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ #>   x= 0, y= 0, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 60, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 120, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 180, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: TRUE  # Add new points tableParam$formula$addPoints(c(1, 2, 3), c(5, 6, 7)) tableParam #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|TableParameter #>   ŌĆó Value: 10.00 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isTable: TRUE #>   ŌĆó XDimension: Time #>   ŌĆó UseDerivedValues: FALSE #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Table values ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ #>   x= 0, y= 0, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 1, y= 5, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 2, y= 6, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 3, y= 7, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 60, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 120, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE #>   x= 180, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE #>   ŌĆó Value overrides formula: TRUE  # title: Dimensions and Units
text: Every entity - molecule, parameter, observer - certain dimension, like Amount, Concentration, Volume. dimension property entity: values certain dimension can presented different units - example, l ml dimension Volume, mol ┬Ąmol dimension Amount. list available units entity can obtained using allUnits() method: Internally, OSPS works base units, values shown passed functions base units default. base units often different units displayed default PK-Sim (MoBi). list base default display units can found documentation. example, parameter BMI given default unit kg/dm┬▓, default display unit convenient kg/m┬▓. {ospuite} package provides set methods conversion different units. methods toUnit(), toBaseUnit(), toDisplayUnit() require quantity get correct dimension units; however, change value quantity!
code: library(ospsuite)  # Load a simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # Get the parameter volume of the liver. livParam <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Volume", sim) print(livParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 2.17 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-ICRP, 2002. Basic Anatomical and Physiological #>   Data for Use in Radiological Protection Reference Values. ICRP Publication #>   89. Ann. ICRP 32 (3-4). https://doi.org/10.1016/0146-6453(79)90123-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isDistributed: TRUE  # Dimension of the parameter livParam$dimension #> [1] "Volume" # Dimension of the parameter livParam$dimension #> [1] "Volume"  # Units of the parameter livParam$allUnits #> [1] "l"  "ml" "┬Ąl" # Get the BMI parameter heightParam <- getParameter("Organism|Height", sim) print(heightParam) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Height #>   ŌĆó Value: 17.00 [dm] #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isConstant: TRUE  # Print the base and the default display units heightParam$unit #> [1] "dm" heightParam$displayUnit #> [1] "cm"  # Convert the value from the base into the default display unit toDisplayUnit(quantity = heightParam, values = heightParam$value) #> [1] 170  # Convert the value to the base unit, that can be used. e.g. for setting new parameter value toBaseUnit(quantity = heightParam, values = 180, unit = "cm") #> [1] 18  liverVolume <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Volume", sim) print(liverVolume) #> <Parameter> #>   ŌĆó Quantity Type: Parameter #>   ŌĆó Path: Organism|Liver|Volume #>   ŌĆó Value: 2.17 [l] #>   ŌĆó Value Origin: Publication-ICRP, 2002. Basic Anatomical and Physiological #>   Data for Use in Radiological Protection Reference Values. ICRP Publication #>   89. Ann. ICRP 32 (3-4). https://doi.org/10.1016/0146-6453(79)90123-4 #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ Formula ŌöĆŌöĆ #>  #>   ŌĆó isDistributed: TRUE  liverVolume$allUnits #> [1] "l"  "ml" "┬Ąl"  # Convert from base volume unit to ┬Ąl toUnit(quantity = liverVolume, values = c(1, 2, 3, 4), targetUnit = "ml") #> [1] 1000 2000 3000 4000

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
text: ospsuite R package provides functionality loading, manipulating, simulating pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic (PK/PD) models created Open Systems Pharmacology Software tools (PK-Sim MoBi). binary R package wraps .NET libraries rSharp package. Key characteristics: - Language: R (version 4.1+) - Architecture: R6 class-based object-oriented design wrapping .NET objects - Platforms: Windows Linux (Ubuntu) - Testing: testthat (edition 3) - Documentation: roxygen2 markdown support - Dependencies: Includes binary .NET DLLs, uses rSharp .NET interoperability

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
code: OSPSuite-R/ Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ R/                      # R source code (~89 files) Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ *-base.R           # Base classes (ObjectBase, DotNetWrapper, etc.) Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ *.R                # R6 class definitions and functions Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ utilities-*.R      # Helper utilities for various operations Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ zzz.R              # Package initialization hooks Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ tests/ Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ testthat/          # Unit tests (~87 test files) Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ man/                    # Generated documentation (roxygen2) Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ vignettes/             # Package vignettes (~15 .Rmd files) Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ inst/                   # Installed files (DLLs, example data) Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ BinaryFiles/           # Platform-specific .NET binaries ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ .github/               # GitHub workflows and configurations

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
text: package extensively uses R6 classes wrap .NET objects: NetObject - Base class .NET object wrappers DotNetWrapper - Extends NetObject common wrapper methods ObjectBase - Extends DotNetWrapper OSPSuite.Core objects Simulation, Container, Parameter, Quantity, Molecule DataSet, DataRepository, DataColumn Population, IndividualCharacteristics Use R6::R6Class() cloneable = FALSE .NET-backed objects Active bindings properties (read-read-write) self$ref self$pointer access underlying .NET object rSharp:: functions .NET interoperability

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
code: ClassName <- R6::R6Class(   "ClassName",   cloneable = FALSE,   inherit = ObjectBase,  # or DotNetWrapper   active = list(     #' @field propertyName Description (read-only or read-write)     propertyName = function(value) {       if (missing(value)) {         # Getter         return(self$get("PropertyName"))       } else {         # Setter (if applicable)         validateIsString(value)         self$set("PropertyName", value)       }     }   ),   public = list(     #' @description Constructor     #' @param netObject .NET object reference     initialize = function(netObject) {       # Initialization code     },          #' @description Method description     #' @param arg1 Description     #' @return Return value description     methodName = function(arg1) {       # Method implementation     }   ),   private = list(     # Private fields and methods   ) )

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R

code: simulationResults <- runSimulation(sim) getParameterValue <- function(parameter) { ... } SimulationResults <- R6::R6Class("SimulationResults", ...) MAX_ITERATIONS <- 1000 DEFAULT_TOLERANCE <- 1e-6 private = list(   .cachedValue = NULL,   .internalMethod = function() { ... } )

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
text: Indentation: 2 spaces (tabs) Line length: Keep lines 80-100 characters Assignment: Use <- assignment, = Spacing: Space commas, around operators Function definitions: Opening brace line, closing brace new line Avoid hard-coded values: Use named constants parameters

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
text: Every exported function class must roxygen2 documentation: R6 classes: - Use @field active bindings - Use @description methods - Document constructor parameters initialize()
code: #' @title Brief title #' @description Detailed description of what the function/class does #' #' @param paramName Description of parameter #' @param anotherParam Description #' #' @return Description of return value (type and structure) #' #' @examples #' \dontrun{ #'   result <- functionName(arg1, arg2) #' } #' #' @export functionName <- function(paramName, anotherParam) {   # Implementation }

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
text: Always validate function inputs:
code: # Use validation functions from ospsuite.utils or base R validateIsString(value) validateIsNumeric(value) validateIsOfType(object, "Simulation")  # Or use stopifnot, assertthat, checkmate stopifnot(length(values) > 0)

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
text: Error messages defined R/messages.R ensure consistency reusability. error messages already defined ospsuite.utils package - see ospsuite.utils messages.R. Logging functionality: Error, debug, info messages can additionally logged using ospsuite.utils logging functionality. See logger documentation details. Message formatting: Error, debug, info messages can formatted using cli enhanced readability. See taking advantage glue cli formatting details.
code: # Define error messages in messages.R messages$errorSimulationIsNull <- function() {   "Simulation cannot be NULL" }  messages$errorDeprecatedFunction <- function(oldFunc, newFunc) {   paste0("Function '", oldFunc, "' is deprecated. Use '", newFunc, "' instead.") }  # Use stop() with messages from messages.R if (is.null(simulation)) {   stop(messages$errorSimulationIsNull()) }  # Use warning() for non-fatal issues with messages from messages.R if (deprecated) {   warning(messages$errorDeprecatedFunction("oldFunction", "newFunction")) }  # Use tryCatch() for operations that may fail result <- tryCatch(   {     riskyOperation()   },   error = function(e) {     stop(paste(messages$failedToPerformOperation(e$message)))   } ) # Example of logging with ospsuite.utils ospsuite.utils::logError(messages$someError) ospsuite.utils::logDebug(messages$someDebugInformation) ospsuite.utils::logInfo(messages$someInformation) # Example of formatting messages with cli # Define messages with cli formatting in messages.R messages$errorLoadingSimulation <- function(filePath) {   "Failed to load simulation from {.file {filePath}}" }  messages$infoProcessingData <- function(nRows) {   "Processing {.val {nRows}} rows of data" }  messages$debugParameterValue <- function(paramName, paramValue) {   "Parameter {.field {paramName}} has value {.val {paramValue}}" }  # Use in code with logging ospsuite.utils::logError(messages$errorLoadingSimulation(filePath)) ospsuite.utils::logInfo(messages$infoProcessingData(nRows)) ospsuite.utils::logDebug(messages$debugParameterValue(paramName, paramValue))

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions for OSPSuite-R
text: Vectorization: Prefer vectorized operations loops Efficient data structures: Use appropriate data structures (data.frame, data.table, lists) Avoid unnecessary copies: R uses copy--modify; mindful large objects

text: Pre-built packages available binary files (.zip Windows, .tar.gz Linux) Download OSPSuite-R binary archive releases page. Download install additional non-CRAN dependencies: {rSharp} {ospuite.utils} {tlf} RStudio IDE, use Install option Packages pane select Install -> Package Archive File install packages binary files. Note: CRAN dependencies rSharp, {ospuite.utils}, tlf installed previous step.
code: # Install rSharp from local file  # Replace pathTo_rSharp.zip with the actual path to the .zip file (or to the .tar.gz file on Linux) install.packages(pathTo_rSharp.zip, repos = NULL)  # Install ospsuite.utils from local file # Replace pathTo_ospsuite.utils.zip with the actual path to the .zip file (or to the .tar.gz file on Linux) install.packages(pathTo_ospsuite.utils.zip, repos = NULL)  # Install tlf from local file # Replace pathTo_tlf.zip with the actual path to the .zip file (or to the .tar.gz file on Linux) install.packages(pathTo_tlf.zip, repos = NULL)  # Install ospsuite from local file # Replace pathToOSPSuite.zip with the actual path to the .zip file (or to the .tar.gz file on Linux) install.packages(pathTo_OSPSuite.zip, repos = NULL)

what: Loading ospsuite might fail if the system locale is not set to English
title: R package to manipulate OSPSuite Models
text: Windows, set Settings > Language > Administrative language settings > Current language non-Unicode programs English (United States) reboot. Linux, set environment variable LC_ALL ~/.bashrc starting R: export LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 add path lib subfolder installed ospsuite package LD_LIBRARY_PATH export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/lib/R/site-library/ospsuite/lib/:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

title: Plot configuration for OSP plots ŌĆö DefaultPlotConfiguration
text: R6 configuration class defining aesthetic properties plots can created plotIndividualTimeProfile(), plotPopulationTimeProfile(), plotObservedVsSimulated(), plotResidualsVsTime(). interactively explore various aesthetic properties appearance plots properties, can use Shiny app {tlf} package. following sections provide details customize .

title: Plot configuration for OSP plots ŌĆö DefaultPlotConfiguration
text: Aesthetic mappings describe groups mapped visual properties (color, shape, size, etc.) geometries included plot (e.g. point, line, ribbon, etc.). supported values property can seen using {tlf} lists: color, fill: tlf::ColorMaps shape: tlf::Shapes legend position: tlf::LegendPositions alignments: tlf::Alignments linetype: tlf::Linetypes example, parameters related color (titleColor, yAxisLabelTicksColor, etc.) accept palettes available tlf::ColorMaps (e.g. tlf::ColorMaps$ospDefault). Note named lists, , therefore, want assign specific element list object's public field, extract element first. example, want specify legend position outside plot left bottom, following:   course, extracted element single value, can also atomic vector. example, want assign different line type group profile plot, assign vector line types.   number elements vector number groups, additional elements ignored.
code: myPlotConfiguration <- DefaultPlotConfiguration$new() myPlotConfiguration$legendPosition <- tlf::LegendPositions$outsideBottomLeft myPlotConfiguration <- DefaultPlotConfiguration$new() myPlotConfiguration$linesLinetype <- names(tlf::Linetypes)

title: Plot configuration for OSP plots ŌĆö DefaultPlotConfiguration

title: SensitivityAnalysis ŌĆö SensitivityAnalysis
text: simulation Reference Simulation used calculate import sensitivity analysis results (Read-). numberOfSteps Number steps used variation parameter one direction reference value. parameter varied positive negative directions, total number variations per parameter 2 * numberOfSteps. example, numberOfSteps = 2 variationRange = 0.1 tests parameter four points: 90%, 95%, 105%, 110% reference value. Default value can retrieved getOSPSuiteSetting("sensitivityAnalysisConfig")$numberOfSteps. variationRange Relative variation range applied parameter. defines total range variation reference value. example, variationRange = 0.1 means ┬▒10% variation. Combined numberOfSteps = 2, parameter tested 90%, 95%, 105%, 110% reference value (.e., variation range divided numberOfSteps equal intervals direction). Default value can retrieved getOSPSuiteSetting("sensitivityAnalysisConfig")$variationRange. parameterPaths List parameters use sensitivity calculation. empty, sensitivity performed automatically constant parameters really use simulation. Constant parameter means parameters constant value formula parameter value overridden user

title: SensitivityAnalysis ŌĆö SensitivityAnalysis
text: simulation Simulation sensitivity analysis performed parameterPaths Vector parameter paths use sensitivity calculation (optional). undefined, sensitivity performed automatically constant parameters simulation. Constant parameter means parameters constant value formula parameter value overridden user numberOfSteps Number steps used variation parameter one direction reference value (optional, default specified getOSPSuiteSetting("sensitivityAnalysisConfig")). parameter varied positive negative directions, total number variations per parameter 2 * numberOfSteps. example, numberOfSteps = 2 variationRange = 0.1, parameter tested four points: 90% (refValue * 0.9), 95% (refValue * 0.95), 105% (refValue * 1.05), 110% (refValue * 1.1) reference value. total number simulations 2 * numberOfSteps * number_of_parameters. variationRange Relative variation range applied parameter (optional, default specified getOSPSuiteSetting("sensitivityAnalysisConfig")). defines total range variation. example, variationRange = 0.1 means ┬▒10% variation. variation range divided numberOfSteps equal intervals direction (positive negative).

code: \dontrun{ sim1 <- loadSimulation("sim1", loadFromCache = TRUE) sim2 <- loadSimulation("sim2", loadFromCache = TRUE) parameters <- c("Organism|Liver|Volume", "R1|k1") molecules <- "Organism|Liver|A" # Create two simulation batches. simulationBatch1 <- createSimulationBatch(simulation = sim1, parametersOrPaths = parameters, moleculesOrPaths = molecules) simulationBatch2 <- createSimulationBatch(simulation = sim2, parametersOrPaths = parameters, moleculesOrPaths = molecules) #Ids of run values ids <- c() ids[[1]] <- simulationBatch1$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(1, 2), initialValues = 1) ids[[2]] <- simulationBatch1$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(1.6, 2.4), initialValues = 3) ids[[3]] <- simulationBatch2$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(4, 2), initialValues = 4) ids[[4]] <- simulationBatch2$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(2.6, 4.4), initialValues = 5) res <- runSimulationBatches(simulationBatches = list(simulationBatch1, simulationBatch2)) }

what: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
title: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
text: Computes residuals observed simulated datasets interpolating simulated values observed time points calculating difference according specified scaling method.

title: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
code: calculateResiduals(dataCombined, scaling, xUnit = NULL, yUnit = NULL)

title: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
text: dataCombined single instance DataCombined class containing observed simulated datasets compared. scaling character specifying scaling method residual calculation. Must either tlf::Scaling$lin linear residuals (simulated - observed) tlf::Scaling$log logarithmic residuals (log(simulated) - log(observed)). xUnit, yUnit Target units xValues yValues, respectively. specified (NULL), first existing unit respective columns selected common unit. available dimensions units, see ospsuite::ospDimensions ospsuite::ospUnits.

title: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
text: tibble (data frame) containing paired observed-simulated data calculated residuals. following columns present: group Grouping identifier original DataCombined object name Name observed dataset nameSimulated Name paired simulated dataset xValues X-axis values (typically time points) observed data xUnit Unit x-values harmonization xDimension Dimension x-values (e.g., "Time") yValuesObserved Observed y-values x-point yValuesSimulated Simulated y-values interpolated observed x-points residualValues Calculated residuals (method depends scaling parameter) yUnit Unit y-values harmonization yDimension Dimension y-values (e.g., "Concentration") yErrorValues Error values observed data (available) yErrorType Type error (e.g., "SD", "SE") yErrorUnit Unit error values lloq Lower limit quantification (available) Returns NULL warning pairable datasets found.

title: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
text: function performs following steps calculate residuals: Data Validation Pairing: group data, function pairs observed datasets simulated datasets. unpaired datasets (observed without corresponding simulated vice versa) removed. Unit Harmonization: datasets converted common units (specified xUnit yUnit parameters) ensure consistent calculations. Interpolation: observed-simulated pair, function uses linear interpolation estimate simulated values exact time points observations exist: 2+ simulated points: Linear interpolation via stats::approx() 1 simulated point: Direct matching identical x-values 0 simulated points: residuals set NA Residual Calculation: Residuals computed based scaling method: Linear scaling (tlf::Scaling$lin): $$residual = y_{simulated} - y_{observed}$$ Logarithmic scaling (tlf::Scaling$log): $$residual = \log(y_{simulated}) - \log(y_{observed})$$ Note: log scaling, small epsilon value used handle zero near-zero values safely.

title: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
text: Residuals can computed observed simulated data exist group Interpolation extrapolate beyond range simulated data (returns NA observed points outside simulated time range) NA residual values automatically filtered output multiple observed/simulated datasets exist group, possible pairs evaluated

title: Calculate residuals for datasets in DataCombined ŌĆö calculateResiduals
code: # simulated data simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) simResults <- runSimulations(sim)[[1]] outputPath <- "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"  # observed data obsData <- lapply(   c("ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_2.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_3.pkml"),   function(x) loadDataSetFromPKML(system.file("extdata", x, package = "ospsuite")) ) names(obsData) <- lapply(obsData, function(x) x$name)   # title: Extract aggregated simulated data ŌĆö .extractAggregatedSimulatedData
text: simData data frame simulated data DataCombined$toDataFrame(). aggregation type aggregation individual data. One quantiles (Default), arithmetic geometric (full list ospsuite::DataAggregationMethods). replace yValues median, arithmetic geometric average add set upper lower bounds (yValuesLower yValuesHigher). ... Arguments passed .normRange nsd optional argument defining number standard deviation add substract mean quantiles numerical vector quantile values (Default: c(0.05, 0.50, 0.95)) plotted. Ignored aggregation quantiles.

title: Extract aggregated simulated data ŌĆö .extractAggregatedSimulatedData
text: simulated values aggregated across individuals time point. aggregation = quantiles (default), quantile values defined argument quantiles used. profile plot, middle value used draw line, lower upper values used lower und upper ranges. aggregation = arithmetic, arithmetic mean arithmetic standard deviation (SD) plotted. Use optional parameter nsd change number SD plot mean. aggregation = geometric, geometric mean geometric standard deviation (SD) plotted. Use optional parameter nsd change number SD plot mean.

title: Convert a data frame to common units ŌĆö .unitConverter
code: # small dataframe to illustrate the conversion (df <- dplyr::tibble(   dataType = c(rep("simulated", 3), rep("observed", 3)),   xValues = c(0, 14.482, 28.965, 0, 1, 2),   xUnit = "min",   xDimension = "Time",   yValues = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1),   yUnit = c("mol", "mol", "mol", "g", "g", "g"),   yDimension = c("Amount", "Amount", "Amount", "Mass", "Mass", "Mass"),   yErrorValues = c(2.747, 2.918, 2.746, NA, NA, NA),   yErrorUnit = c("mol", "mol", "mol", "g", "g", "g"),   molWeight = c(10, 10, 20, 20, 20, 10) )) #> # A tibble: 6 ├Ś 10 #>   dataType  xValues xUnit xDimension yValues yUnit yDimension yErrorValues #>   <chr>       <dbl> <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <chr> <chr>             <dbl> #> 1 simulated     0   min   Time             1 mol   Amount             2.75 #> 2 simulated    14.5 min   Time             1 mol   Amount             2.92 #> 3 simulated    29.0 min   Time             1 mol   Amount             2.75 #> 4 observed      0   min   Time             1 g     Mass              NA    #> 5 observed      1   min   Time             1 g     Mass              NA    #> 6 observed      2   min   Time             1 g     Mass              NA    #> # Ōä╣ 2 more variables: yErrorUnit <chr>, molWeight <dbl>  # default conversion ospsuite:::.unitConverter(df) #> # A tibble: 6 ├Ś 10 #>   dataType  xValues xUnit xDimension yValues yUnit yDimension yErrorValues #>   <chr>       <dbl> <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <chr> <chr>             <dbl> #> 1 simulated     0   min   Time            10 g     Amount             27.5 #> 2 simulated    14.5 min   Time            10 g     Amount             29.2 #> 3 simulated    29.0 min   Time            20 g     Amount             54.9 #> 4 observed      0   min   Time             1 g     Mass               NA   #> 5 observed      1   min   Time             1 g     Mass               NA   #> 6 observed      2   min   Time             1 g     Mass               NA   #> # Ōä╣ 2 more variables: yErrorUnit <chr>, molWeight <dbl>  # customizing conversion with specified unit(s) ospsuite:::.unitConverter(df, xUnit = ospUnits$Time$h) #> # A tibble: 6 ├Ś 10 #>   dataType  xValues xUnit xDimension yValues yUnit yDimension yErrorValues #>   <chr>       <dbl> <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <chr> <chr>             <dbl> #> 1 simulated  0      h     Time            10 g     Amount             27.5 #> 2 simulated  0.241  h     Time            10 g     Amount             29.2 #> 3 simulated  0.483  h     Time            20 g     Amount             54.9 #> 4 observed   0      h     Time             1 g     Mass               NA   #> 5 observed   0.0167 h     Time             1 g     Mass               NA   #> 6 observed   0.0333 h     Time             1 g     Mass               NA   #> # Ōä╣ 2 more variables: yErrorUnit <chr>, molWeight <dbl> ospsuite:::.unitConverter(df, yUnit = ospUnits$Mass$kg) #> # A tibble: 6 ├Ś 10 #>   dataType  xValues xUnit xDimension yValues yUnit yDimension yErrorValues #>   <chr>       <dbl> <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <chr> <chr>             <dbl> #> 1 simulated     0   min   Time         0.01  kg    Amount           0.0275 #> 2 simulated    14.5 min   Time         0.01  kg    Amount           0.0292 #> 3 simulated    29.0 min   Time         0.02  kg    Amount           0.0549 #> 4 observed      0   min   Time         0.001 kg    Mass            NA      #> 5 observed      1   min   Time         0.001 kg    Mass            NA      #> 6 observed      2   min   Time         0.001 kg    Mass            NA      #> # Ōä╣ 2 more variables: yErrorUnit <chr>, molWeight <dbl> ospsuite:::.unitConverter(df, xUnit = ospUnits$Time$s, yUnit = ospUnits$Amount$mmol) #> # A tibble: 6 ├Ś 10 #>   dataType  xValues xUnit xDimension yValues yUnit yDimension yErrorValues #>   <chr>       <dbl> <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <chr> <chr>             <dbl> #> 1 simulated      0  s     Time          1000 mmol  Amount             2747 #> 2 simulated    869. s     Time          1000 mmol  Amount             2918 #> 3 simulated   1738. s     Time          1000 mmol  Amount             2746 #> 4 observed       0  s     Time            50 mmol  Mass                 NA #> 5 observed      60  s     Time            50 mmol  Mass                 NA #> 6 observed     120  s     Time           100 mmol  Mass                 NA #> # code: exportSteadyStateToXLS(   simulation,   quantitiesPaths = NULL,   resultsXLSPath = "",   steadyStateTime = NULL,   ignoreIfFormula = TRUE,   lowerThreshold = 1e-15,   simulationRunOptions = NULL )

title: Export steady-state to Excel in the format that can be imported in MoBi. ŌĆö exportSteadyStateToXLS
text: simulation Simulation object steady-state simulated. contrast getSteadyState(), one simulation supported. quantitiesPaths List quantity paths (molecules /parameters) steady-state simulated. NULL (default), molecules state variable parameters considered. list applied simulations. resultsXLSPath Path xls-file results written . file exist, new file created. path provided, file created directory model file located. name file <SimulationFileName>_SS.xlsx. steadyStateTime Simulation time (minutes). NULL (default), default simulation time start time last application plus three days. simulated time must long enough system reach steady-state. Either single value (applied simulations), list values specific simulation. latter case, must equal size simulations. providing list, NULL allowed calculate time based last application. ignoreIfFormula TRUE (default), species parameters initial values defined formula included. lowerThreshold Numerical value (default unit output). steady-state values absolute value threshold considered numerical noise replaced 0 (.e., values interval [-lowerThreshold, lowerThreshold]). lowerThreshold NULL, cut-applied. Default value 1e-15. simulationRunOptions Optional instance SimulationRunOptions used simulation run.

title: Get the steady-state values of species and state variable parameters. ŌĆö getSteadyState
code: getSteadyState(   simulations,   quantitiesPaths = NULL,   steadyStateTime = NULL,   ignoreIfFormula = TRUE,   lowerThreshold = 1e-15,   simulationRunOptions = NULL )

title: Get the steady-state values of species and state variable parameters. ŌĆö getSteadyState
text: simulations Simulation object list vector Simulation objects simulate. List vector can named (names must uniques), case names reused output list. named, output list use simulation ids names. quantitiesPaths List quantity paths (molecules /parameters) steady-state simulated. NULL (default), molecules state variable parameters considered. list applied simulations. steadyStateTime Simulation time (minutes). NULL (default), default simulation time start time last application plus three days. simulated time must long enough system reach steady-state. Either single value (applied simulations), list values specific simulation. latter case, must equal size simulations. providing list, NULL allowed calculate time based last application. ignoreIfFormula TRUE (default), species parameters initial values defined formula included. lowerThreshold Numerical value (default unit output). steady-state values absolute value threshold considered numerical noise replaced 0 (.e., values interval [-lowerThreshold, lowerThreshold]). lowerThreshold NULL, cut-applied. Default value 1e-15. simulationRunOptions Optional instance SimulationRunOptions used simulation run.

title: Get the steady-state values of species and state variable parameters. ŌĆö getSteadyState
text: named list, names IDs simulations entries lists containing paths values end simulation.

title: Get the steady-state values of species and state variable parameters. ŌĆö getSteadyState
text: steady-state considered last values molecules amounts state variable parameters simulation sufficiently long simulation time, .e., rates processes (significantly) change. steady-state analytically calculated estimated way simulating given time.

title: Get the steady-state values of species and state variable parameters. ŌĆö getSteadyState
code: simPath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simPath) steadyState <- getSteadyState(simulations = sim) # Set initial values for steady-state simulations setQuantityValuesByPath(   quantityPaths = steadyState[[sim$id]]$paths,   values = steadyState[[sim$id]]$values, simulation = sim )

text: filePath Full path pkml simulation file load. loadFromCache TRUE, already loaded pkml file loaded , simulation object retrieved cache. FALSE, new simulation object created. title: Load a simulation from a pkml file ŌĆö loadSimulation
code: simPath <- system.file("extdata", "simple.pkml", package = "ospsuite")  # Load sim1 for the first time sim1 <- loadSimulation(simPath)  # sim2 will be loaded from cache and will represent the same object as sim1 sim2 <- loadSimulation(simPath, loadFromCache = TRUE)  parameter1 <- getParameter(toPathString(c("Organism", "Liver", "Volume")), sim1) parameter2 <- getParameter(toPathString(c("Organism", "Liver", "Volume")), sim2)  # parameter1 and parameter2 belong to the same simulation object, so changing # one of the them will also change another setParameterValues(parameters = parameter2, values = 0) parameter1$value == parameter2$value # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  # sim3 will not be loaded from cache sim3 <- loadSimulation(simPath, loadFromCache = FALSE) # parameter3 belong to different simulation object than parameter1 and parameter2 parameter3 <- getParameter(toPathString(c("Organism", "Liver", "Volume")), sim3) setParameterValues(parameters = parameter3, values = 1) parameter2$value == parameter3$value # FALSE#' #> [1] FALSE

title: Time-profile plot of individual data ŌĆö plotIndividualTimeProfile
code: # simulated data simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) simResults <- runSimulations(sim)[[1]] outputPath <- "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"  # observed data obsData <- lapply(   c("ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_2.pkml", "ObsDataAciclovir_3.pkml"),   function(x) loadDataSetFromPKML(system.file("extdata", x, package = "ospsuite")) ) names(obsData) <- lapply(obsData, function(x) x$name)   # Create a new instance of `DataCombined` class myDataCombined <- DataCombined$new()  # Add simulated results myDataCombined$addSimulationResults(   simulationResults = simResults,   quantitiesOrPaths = outputPath,   groups = "Aciclovir PVB" )  # Add observed data set myDataCombined$addDataSets(obsData$`Vergin 1995.Iv`, groups = "Aciclovir PVB")  # Create a new instance of `DefaultPlotConfiguration` class myPlotConfiguration <- DefaultPlotConfiguration$new() myPlotConfiguration$title <- "My Plot Title" myPlotConfiguration$subtitle <- "My Plot Subtitle" myPlotConfiguration$caption <- "My Sources"  # plot plotIndividualTimeProfile(myDataCombined, myPlotConfiguration)

title: Run simulation batches ŌĆö runSimulationBatches
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim1 <- loadSimulation("sim1", loadFromCache = TRUE) sim2 <- loadSimulation("sim2", loadFromCache = TRUE) parameters <- c("Organism|Liver|Volume", "R1|k1") molecules <- "Organism|Liver|A" # Create two simulation batches. simulationBatch1 <- createSimulationBatch(   simulation = sim1,   parametersOrPaths = parameters,   moleculesOrPaths = molecules ) simulationBatch2 <- createSimulationBatch(   simulation = sim2,   parametersOrPaths = parameters,   moleculesOrPaths = molecules ) # Ids of run values ids <- c() ids[[1]] <- simulationBatch1$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(1, 2), initialValues = 1) ids[[2]] <- simulationBatch1$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(1.6, 2.4), initialValues = 3) ids[[3]] <- simulationBatch2$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(4, 2), initialValues = 4) ids[[4]] <- simulationBatch2$addRunValues(parameterValues = c(2.6, 4.4), initialValues = 5) res <- runSimulationBatches(simulationBatches = list(simulationBatch1, simulationBatch2)) } # }

List vector can named (names must uniques), case names reused simulationResults output list. named, output list use simulation ids names. population Optional instance Population use simulation. allowed simulating one simulation. Alternatively, can also pass result createPopulation directly. case, population extracted. agingData Optional instance AgingData use simulation. used population simulation simulationRunOptions Optional instance SimulationRunOptions used simulation run silentMode TRUE, warnings displayed simulation fails. Default FALSE. effect stopIfFails TRUE. stopIfFails Whether stop execution one simulations failed. Default FALSE.

title: Runs multiple simulations concurrently. ŌĆö runSimulations
text: named list SimulationResults objects names IDs respective simulations. simulation fails, result simulation NULL

title: Runs multiple simulations concurrently. ŌĆö runSimulations
text: multiple simulations, individual simulations possible. single simulation, either individual population simulations can performed.

title: Runs multiple simulations concurrently. ŌĆö runSimulations
code: simPath <- system.file("extdata", "simple.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simPath)  # Running an individual simulation # Results is a list with one object `SimulationResults` results <- runSimulations(sim)  # Creating custom simulation run options  simRunOptions <- SimulationRunOptions$new() simRunOptions$numberOfCores <- 3 simRunOptions$showProgress <- TRUE  # Running a population simulation popPath <- system.file("extdata", "pop.csv", package = "ospsuite") population <- loadPopulation(popPath) results <- runSimulations(sim, population, simulationRunOptions = simRunOptions)[[1]]  # Running multiple simulations in parallel sim2 <- loadSimulation(simPath) sim3 <- loadSimulation(simPath)  # Results is a list of `SimulationResults` results <- runSimulations(list(sim, sim2, sim3))

title: Converts a value given in base unit of a quantity into a target unit ŌĆö toUnit
text: quantityOrDimension Instance quantity dimension retrieved name dimension values Values convert (single vector). sourceUnit specified, values base unit dimension targetUnit Unit convert sourceUnit Optional Name unit convert . NULL (default), values assumed base unit. molWeight Optional molecular weight use converting, example,  molar mass amount concentration. molWeightUnit specified, molWeight assumed kg/┬Ąmol molWeightUnit Optional Unit molecular weight value. NULL (default), kg/┬Ąmol assumed.

title: Converts a value given in base unit of a quantity into a target unit ŌĆö toUnit
code: simPath <- system.file("extdata", "simple.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simPath) par <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Volume", sim)  # Converts the value in base unit (1L) to ml => 1000 valueInMl <- toUnit(par, 1, "ml")  valuesInMl <- toUnit(par, c(1, 5, 5), "ml")  # Converts a numerical value in from mmol/l to mg/dl valuesInMgDl <- toUnit(ospDimensions$`Concentration (molar)`, 5,   targetUnit = "mmol/l",   sourceUnit = "mg/dl", molWeight = 180, molWeightUnit = "g/mol" )
